title: Add spring-based entrance animations to GanttChart blocks using project motion system
description: GanttChart (VA2=0/3) has ZERO animation — bars appear instantly. The project has a world-class motion system at src/lib/constants/motion.ts with named springs (snappy, smooth, bouncy) and pre-built animations, but OSModule doesn't import it. Add: staggered entrance animation for Gantt blocks (each bar slides in from left with springs.smooth), hover scale effect on each block, and current-step pulse during step-through.
acceptanceCriteria: Import springs from @/lib/constants/motion and wrap GanttChart blocks in motion.div
Blocks animate in with staggered delay: each block enters 50ms after the previous
Use springs.smooth for entrance (not linear easing)
Hover: block scales to 1.02 with springs.snappy
Step-through mode: current block pulses with gentle opacity animation
Respects prefers-reduced-motion (no animation when reduced motion preferred)

title: Add edge-drawing and cycle-pulse animation to Resource Allocation Graph
description: RAG SVG (VA2=0/3) renders instantly. Add: edges draw themselves with SVG stroke-dasharray animation (line extends from source to target over 300ms). Cycle edges pulse red when deadlock is detected. Process nodes enter with scale animation. Use SVG SMIL or CSS animations (not motion/react, which doesn't work well with SVG paths).
acceptanceCriteria: Edges animate drawing: stroke-dashoffset from full length to 0 over 300ms
Cycle edges pulse: red glow animation repeating 3 times when deadlock detected
Process/resource nodes enter with scale 0→1 animation (staggered 100ms each)
Non-cycle edges remain static after initial draw
Animation only on first render or state change, not on every frame

title: Add state transition animations to ThreadSyncCanvas — acquire glow, block fade, release flash
description: ThreadSync (VA2=0/3) has instant state changes despite step-through. Add: acquire = green glow radiating from thread circle. Block = amber pulse and arrow becomes dashed with animation. Release = brief white flash on thread, arrow fades out. Idle = gentle opacity reduction. Arrow drawing animation on state change.
acceptanceCriteria: Acquire: green glow animation (SVG filter or circle scale) lasting 400ms
Block: amber pulse on circle, arrow dasharray animates
Release: white flash + arrow fade out over 200ms
State color transitions use CSS transitions (300ms) not instant
Play mode (500ms interval): animations complete before next state change

title: Add step-by-step frame transition animation to PageFrameGrid
description: PageFrameGrid (VA2=0/3) renders frames instantly. Add: on step advance, new page slides into frame from top. Evicted page slides out to bottom with fade. Hit frame flashes green briefly. Fault frame flashes red briefly. Current column gets a subtle glow background.
acceptanceCriteria: New page value slides in from top of frame cell (transform translateY -20px → 0)
Evicted page value slides out to bottom (translateY 0 → 20px + fade)
Hit: green flash pulse on frame cell lasting 300ms
Fault: red flash on entire column lasting 300ms
Current step column has subtle glow/shadow
Animations complete within 400ms to not block step-through pace

title: Upgrade GanttChart with gradients, depth, and intermediate time ticks
description: GanttChart (VA1=1, VA3=1, VA5=0) looks like flat colored blocks. Upgrade: blocks get subtle gradients (lighter at top, darker at bottom for depth). Rounded corners increased from 2px to 4px. Add 4-6 intermediate time tick marks on the axis. Add vertical gridlines at tick marks. Add hover tooltip showing process details. Idle blocks get diagonal stripes pattern instead of flat gray.
acceptanceCriteria: Block gradient: top 20% lighter, bottom 20% darker (CSS linear-gradient)
Rounded corners: rounded-md (6px) instead of rounded-sm (2px)
Time axis: 4-6 evenly spaced tick marks with labels
Subtle vertical gridlines at each tick mark (1px dashed, 5% opacity)
Hover: block expands slightly (scale 1.02) and shows tooltip with process details
Idle blocks: diagonal stripe pattern using CSS repeating-linear-gradient

title: Upgrade Memory Allocation Bar with block labels, rounded segments, and allocation animation
description: Memory bar (VA2=1) has basic CSS transitions but no visual polish. Upgrade: blocks get rounded inner corners when adjacent to free space. Allocated blocks show process label + size inside. Free blocks show 'Free: {size}B' label. Add allocation animation: new block slides in from the boundary of the free block it was carved from. Add deallocation: block dissolves to free space with fade.
acceptanceCriteria: Block labels always visible (not just when widthPct > 6%): use rotated text for narrow blocks
Allocation: new block slides in from left edge of the free block it was carved from
Deallocation: block fades to gray over 300ms, then merges with adjacent free blocks
Free blocks have subtle diagonal stripe pattern to distinguish from 'empty'
Address markers along the bottom edge at each block boundary

title: Wire GCPauseLatencyVisualizer to use project motion constants instead of raw motion.div
description: GC Visualizer uses motion.div with default easing. The project has springs.smooth, springs.bouncy, duration.fast etc. at src/lib/constants/motion.ts. Wire: timeline bar entrance uses springs.smooth. Button press uses springs.snappy. Heap bar updates use duration.moderate with easing.out.
acceptanceCriteria: Import springs, duration, easing from @/lib/constants/motion
Timeline bars: transition={{ ...springs.smooth }}
Buttons: whileTap={{ scale: 0.97, transition: springs.snappy }}
Heap bars: transition={{ duration: duration.moderate, ease: easing.out }}
Visual result: slightly springier feel, consistent with other animated modules

title: Add address translation journey animation to Memory concept
description: Memory translation (VA2=0) shows the badge chain VA→Page→Offset→Frame→PA as static badges. Redesign as an animated journey: VA badge appears → arrow draws to Page badge → arrow draws to 'TLB Check' node → branch animation to TLB HIT (green path) or TLB MISS → PT check → frame resolution → PA. This turns a static result into a visual story of how address translation FLOWS through hardware.
acceptanceCriteria: Translation shown as an animated flowchart, not static badge chain
Each node appears in sequence (200ms apart) with connecting arrows drawing
TLB check node: green branch on HIT, red branch on MISS → Page Table
Page fault: red terminal node with disk icon
Final PA highlighted with glow effect
Step-through mode: each translation triggers the journey animation

title: Replace all sub-11px text in visualizations with minimum 11px and proper font hierarchy
description: 114 instances of text-[8px] and text-[9px] across all visualizations make labels unreadable. Establish font hierarchy: primary values at 13px (font-semibold), secondary labels at 11px (font-medium), tertiary metadata at 10px (font-normal). Never below 10px. SVG text minimum 11px. Ensure font-mono for numbers, font-sans for labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero instances of text-[8px] or text-[9px] in OS module files
Gantt axis: minimum 10px
SVG legends: minimum 11px
Frame grid labels: minimum 10px
All values use font-mono, all labels use font-sans
Visual hierarchy maintained: values > labels > metadata

title: Design and implement Architex OS signature visual style — gradients, glows, depth
description: The 'screenshot test' fails: no visualization is distinctive enough to recognize as Architex. Design a signature style: (1) Subtle gradient backgrounds on visualization containers (not flat bg-elevated). (2) Active elements get a soft glow (box-shadow with primary color at 10% opacity). (3) Data elements have micro-depth (1px shadow or gradient edge). (4) State colors use gradients (green glow for success, not flat green). (5) Consistent rounded corners (8px for containers, 4px for data elements). Apply to ALL 12 visualizations.
acceptanceCriteria: Visualization containers have subtle gradient background (not flat)
Active/current elements have soft glow (box-shadow: 0 0 12px var(--primary)/20)
Data elements (Gantt blocks, frame cells, memory blocks) have micro-depth
Success/error states use gradient backgrounds, not flat colors
Rounded corners standardized: 8px containers, 4px elements
Result: a screenshot of any visualization is recognizably 'Architex'

title: Add smooth empty state illustrations with animated icons for each concept
description: All 6 concepts show identical generic text 'Configure and press Run.' No illustrations, no visual interest. Design concept-specific empty states: CPU Scheduling: animated CPU icon with idle spinner. Page Replacement: book/shelf illustration. Deadlock: two crossed arrows. Memory: address space diagram outline. Mem Alloc: empty memory bar with 'click to fill' prompt. Thread Sync: idle thread circles.
acceptanceCriteria: Each concept has a unique empty state illustration (SVG or motion component)
Illustrations are subtle and themed (not garish)
Prompt text is action-oriented: 'Add processes and press Run to visualize scheduling'
Illustration has gentle idle animation (breathing scale or soft pulse)
'Try Example' button loads a preset and runs it immediately

title: Add comparison bar chart animations to scheduling and page replacement comparison panels
description: Comparison panels (VA2=0) show bar charts that appear instantly. Add: bars grow from left to right with staggered animation (first bar starts immediately, each subsequent bar starts 100ms later). 'BEST' label entrance with scale bounce. Metrics table rows enter with subtle fade from top.
acceptanceCriteria: Bar chart bars animate width from 0% to final value
Staggered: each bar starts 100ms after the previous
Use springs.smooth for bar growth animation
'BEST' badge enters with springs.bouncy scale animation
Table rows fade in with 50ms stagger
Works in both scheduling comparison and page replacement comparison
